title: Boat Rental in St Lucia: Your 2026 Guide to Island-Hopping by Sea
html:
Exploring St Lucia by boat is the definitive way to experience its twin Pitons, secluded coves, and vibrant marine life. With a private charter starting from around EUR 600 per day, visitors can set off from Rodney Bay Marina to discover the island's Caribbean charm. BoatHire24 simplifies the process of comparing and booking your ideal vessel.

Where to set off in St Lucia

St Lucia offers several well-equipped marinas and natural harbours. Rodney Bay Marina is the largest, with full facilities and proximity to Castries. Marigot Bay provides a sheltered, scenic starting point, while Soufrière Yacht Harbour lies near the Pitons. For a more relaxed departure, consider Vieux Fort or the anchorage at Anse des Sables. Each location gives access to different stretches of coastline.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat, great for coastal cruises and water sports, up to 6 guests, from EUR 500 per half-day.
  2. Catamaran, spacious for group charters, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,200 for a full-day trip.
  3. Sailing yacht, a classic way to explore, accommodating 4, 6 guests, from EUR 900 per day.
  4. RIB, fast and agile for fishing or island hopping, 4, 8 guests, from EUR 400 per half-day. For example, the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire is a popular choice near the Pitons.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in St Lucia?

Renting a boat in St Lucia costs between EUR 200 for a shared tour and EUR 3,500 per week for a crewed yacht. A typical price breakdown includes:

  • Shared group excursion: EUR 200, 300 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 500, 700
  • Catamaran full-day charter: EUR 1,200, 1,800
  • Private crewed yacht per week: EUR 2,500, 3,500

Many boat owners offer discounts for multi-day bookings. To explore current options, you can hire a boat directly on the platform, which lists verified vessels with transparent pricing.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in St Lucia?

In St Lucia, you generally need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent to charter a bareboat without a skipper. Most private boat owners include a skipper in the rental fee, so no licence is required for those charters. The operator will confirm exact requirements at booking. BoatHire24 lists both bareboat and skippered options, so you can choose accordingly.

To avoid peak crowds and trade winds, plan your trip between December and April; the afternoon breeze (the 'doctor wind') picks up around 2pm, so schedule morning departures for calmer seas.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 200, 3,500 per day
  • Peak months: December, April
  • Trip length: 4 hours to full-day
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 persons
  • Currency: EUR (also USD accepted locally)

Questions about boat rental in St Lucia

Do I need a skipper to rent a boat in St Lucia?

Many charters on BoatHire24 include a skipper, which is advisable unless you hold an ICC and are confident in local waters.

How long can I rent a boat for in St Lucia?

Rental durations range from half-day (4 hours) to full-day and multi-day charters.

Is it safe to anchor near the Pitons?

Yes, the waters near Soufrière are sheltered, but always use a mooring buoy to protect the reef and avoid strong gusts from the Gros Piton trail.

What is the best season for sailing in St Lucia?

The high season (December to April) offers steady trade winds and minimal rain, ideal for island-hopping between St Lucia and the Grenadines.

Once you have chosen your vessel, you can review exactly how boat rental works to secure your booking with confidence. For owners, if you have a boat in St Lucia, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 adventure; you can rent out your boat easily through the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in St Lucia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
